name: Vietnamese Curry (Cari)
recipeIngredient: Fresh lemon grass stalks or Dried lemon grass (1 tbsp)
Potatoes (Sweet potatoes or White potatoes, peeled and cubed) (3)
recipeInstructions:
If you use canned coconut milk, you must use water.
This is a real Vietnamese curry. Although adapted from the Indian,
which is always made with white potatoes, the Vietnamese version has
the option of using white or sweet potatoes, the latter being greatly
favored by the Vietnamese. The Indian influence is greatest in the
South, where curried dishes are more popular than elsewhere in
Vietnam.
This is usually served with noodles as a party dish. When it is part
of a family meal, it is eaten with rice. Bach serves this to her
children for breakfast, when it is served with French bread--another
influence on the cuisine of Vietnam.
If you are using fresh lemon grass:
- Remove the outer leaves and upper two-thirds of the stalks.
- Cut the remainder into 2-inch lengths.
- Soak in warm water for 2 hours, then drain and chop fine.
1. Combine curry powder, black pepper, sugar, and salt. Marinate chicken in the mixture for at least 1 hour.
2. Heat oil and fry potatoes until brown (do not cook completely). Remove from pan and set aside.
3. Pour off most of the oil, leaving 2 tablespoons.
4. Heat remaining oil over high flame. Fry garlic briefly, add bay leaves, onion, and lemon grass; stir briefly.
5. Add marinated chicken, water, and carrot. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes; uncover and stir, then cook uncovered for another 10 minutes.
6. Remove cover and add potatoes, coconut milk, and milk. Return cover and simmer for an additional 15 minutes.
7. Serve with rice, rice sticks, or Japanese Alimentary Paste Noodles.
